Hey, welcome back. Alright, so what we're about to do now is go through a high ticket sales process. And generally speaking, high ticket sales processes always involve someone either A, filling out an application, and then B, getting on the phone with you or your client, or just scheduling a call with you or your client without filling out an application. But at the end of the day, the goal of a high ticket funnel or sales process, at least in the online area, is to get someone who's qualified, who knows what to do, who likes you, who believes that you can help them, and to get them to request a conversation with you. So in our next video, I'm going to go through like a super hardcore deep dive of how to really trick out this process. But what I want to give you in this one is a kind of like a shortcut version. This is a never fail thing number one we always do for clients that have a high ticket funnel. And it's two email sequences. And one of them is called a content sequence. And the other one is called a call me sequence. So what I'm going to do, and by the way, I hope the audio is not too weird right now. I got the AC going is 1000 degrees. So I don't want to be sweating on camera for you. So what I've got on my screen here is I did the old split screen trick. And I'm going to show you the process map for this. And I'm also going to show you the emails and examples of the web pages for the content sequence. So first thing you got to realize is the purpose of the content sequence is to get someone to behaviorally indicate that they are interested in getting the results that you can help them get. All right. So that's what we're trying to do. We want them to click. So content sequence is just a fancy way of saying a quick sequence and it's driving the people or driving people to a web page that's got content on it. Now, before I go into the emails, I want to tell you what the web page needs to look like. Okay. So this is like my web pages are terrible looking. I did this one myself. But as you can see on the screen here, this web page is it has a headline. This is just a video here. This is a straight up YouTube video is 30 minutes long. I'm just teaching something about what they want to accomplish. And then under the video, I've got some copy this telling them to book a call. All right. And that's the entire web page. So if you do this method, the idea behind it is you don't just send a quick sequence driving them to one web page with one piece of content. You take a ton of pieces of content, make a web page for each one as a headline video and then some copy about why they should book a call. And then you just put all of those emails into one sequence kind of like this. So I have one that we use and here is the process map for it. So first of all, this is a PDF that's downloadable for you. All right. So essentially the email sequence works like this. We got email number one. Hey, go watch this video. It shows you how to do some stuff that you want to do. If they click it and they book a call, then we don't email them anymore. All right. If they click it and they don't book a call, we send them a second type of campaign that's called a call me campaign. But we also don't take them out of this email sequence. We send them, if we have another piece of content, we'll send them that next piece of content in another email tomorrow or the day after. So it's entirely possible that somebody who's going through this can be getting content emails and emails that are saying, hey man, give me a call all at the same time. So these emails, these content emails are always driving to web pages like this one where you got a web page with a video on it. All right. You got the video itself. It teaches them how to do something that they want to do. And under the video is a link to book a call or apply. So in hours when you book a call, we're using a calendar here, you can actually have your application questions in the scheduler. So it's the same thing. You know, we just kind of kill two birds with one stone there. But you get it. All right. So before I go over these actual content emails, because they're super simple, just know that every single web page they're going to. All right. So here's another one, by the way. Let's have a look at this one. Just to show you. This is a different email right here. And I'm going to bring up a better version of these emails. But look, this is a different video, but same layout. Right? So we got headline, video, totally different content. That last one was 30 minutes. This one's 10. All right. And then same copy saying, hey, you know, book a call with my team. Now, if they click on any of that stuff, all right, I've got to fix my computer here. If they click on any of these emails, then they are simultaneously put into a call me campaign. And a call me campaign is simply three emails sent one every day, the same, hey, go here and book a call with me. All right. And so this one, I don't have the hyperlinks not active here. But this one's literally just going to my homepage, rowas.org. And people can just book a call right there on the page. So it's exactly like, where'd you go? So just so you know that there's nothing like, you know, really crazy or anything there is the same copy, same everything, different video. All right. So that's what a call me sequence is. So these guys work. Whoops. There we go. These two work together in synergy. So and they're simultaneously, so nothing removes them from the content click sequence, all right? The only thing that's going to stop the content thing or the call me campaign is if they actually book a call. So let's kind of take a look at these, right? In terms of the call me emails here. So number one is please call me. This is the first thing we'll do for a client if they have a bunch of people that know them and they want to generate appointments is we'll just send the call me campaign first without even building the click sequence. We'll just be like, all right, let's do a call me campaign and see what happens because we want to get our clients money as soon as possible. So if you need appointments and you have a list and they know you, you might want to just fire up one of these call me campaigns and send it out. So this one is like, hey, please call me. And the framework has dropped me in line. If you want us to do any of these things for you. So let's say you're a weight loss coach, right? Yours could be, hey, please call me. Drop me a line if you want me to do any of these things for you. Number one, help you get an incredible shape. Number two, help you burn fat no matter what you eat. Number three, help you do all of this without losing any muscle, whatever. So it's basically drop me a line if you want me to do one, two or three of these cool things for you, right? And then I say, okay, or do any of these things with you because we also do some cool training. Here's how to reach me, all right? And this goes again, this goes just to my homepage here, all right? The page, and you describe your page here. I mean, as I showed you my homepage, it literally just has like three sentences. So, so go here and check out the three sentence page and you can book a call, all right? And then the second email here is essentially like, you know, an even shorter version, all right? Frank, getting help. Hey, Frank. Do you have any questions about how your offer works or need help in figuring out if it makes sense to get offer? All right? So this is actually a template that I put in here for you, all right? So again, like let's say that you're selling weight loss coaching, all right? And in this case, it would be, hey, do you have any questions about how our weight loss coaching works or do you need help in figuring out if it makes sense to get coached in terms of hitting your ideal weight? We're happy to help you. We'll choose a time that works best for you and we'll do a quick one-on-one call to help you. You can do that here and that would be a link to your calendar and then you can put your signature line here, all right? And here is an example from mine, which is, hey, want me to take care of this for you? Now notice the similarities, all right, between email number one here and email number three for me. I'd love to use the same copy over and over again. So instead of dropping me a line, I'm just literally saying everything's the same here. I'm just saying, want us to do your marketing for you? This is what we can do for you. All that same stuff. I said pretty much the same. Yeah, it's the same stuff, all right? And then go here and click it. Okay, so really, really basic to the point. It's like, if you want me to do this for you, go here and let's talk. Really simple, all right? Or if you have questions about this stuff, go here and let's talk and see if it makes sense. Really, really basic, all right? So that is how a content to call me sequence works. And if you sell stuff over the phone and you want to generate appointments and you've got a list right now, here's exactly what you should do. Drop everything. Number one, just send out a call me campaign, right? Like three emails, basically tell them to call you and here's how I can help you. Go here and book a call with me. And then while you're doing that, go look at some of your past content, any Facebook lives, YouTube videos you've done or whatever. And slap them on a webpage with a headline over it and stick your calendar underneath that sucker and then go out and send a sequence that drives people to those web pages. And when they click it, pop them into that call me campaign. You will get phone calls, assuming you're people like you and want to talk to you. It's a really, really good, just super fast, down and dirty, quick fix, hyper effective way to get people on the phone. ASAP. So I wanted to show you that. And on our next video, we're going to go like hogwound crazy in terms of really how to maximize a phone call funnel using webinars and stuff. So if you have the appropriate funnel, deploy this immediately and I'll see you in the next video regardless. All right.
